name: Sekiro™: Shadows Die Twice - GOTY Edition
description: Game of the Year - The Game Awards 2019 Best Action Game of 2019 - IGN Carve your own clever path to vengeance in the award winning adventure from developer FromSoftware, creators of Bloodborne and the Dark Souls series. Take Revenge. Restore Your Honor. Kill Ingeniously.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: Windows 7 64-bit | Windows 8 64-bit | Windows 10 64-bit
  • Processor: Intel Core i3-2100 | AMD FX-6300
  • Memory: 4 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 760 | AMD Radeon HD 7950
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 25 GB available space
  • Sound Card: DirectX 11 Compatible
comment1: Lives upto to its legendary status, had to get gud IRL. Combat never felt this satisfying.
comment2:
For a game that is easily 10/10 on a normal day, except for the day you have to "subdue" the burning bull.. The flames on its horns are foreshadowing for what you are about to experience, you shall burn in the deepest pit of hell. I hope the person that made the bull gets paid minimum wage. After playthrough #2 its not even a viable option to fight the bull anymore, this bull will single handedly make you regret the day you bought this game with the extra hp, posture and the damage, MY GOD THE DAMAGE combined with a hitbox that made me question my sanity. Around the end game theres a boss called demon of hatred, they should replace that boss with a bull and 2 extra lives, immediately a million times harder to beat, the game goes on and on about discipline on how you need to be patient and deflect all the enemy attacks, meanwhile the bull is like an actual brick wall and instead of the wall taking gradual damage, its just you getting proneboned where you either have to run around like an idiot and hit once every blue moon or just skip it if you dont wanna waste an hour of your life (unless its playthrough #1 without debuffs). 100 hours into the game, i could beat any boss without even attacking meanwhile this boss makes me gather more sweat in my ass crack than a 104 aussie summer.
definitely recommend the game if youre planning to skip the boss.
comment3:
Sekiro was the first Fromsoftware game I ever played, yeaaaa it was rough. Slowly but surely I built up skill in the game, but often times id get past the bosses through sheer willingness to throw myself at a brick wall until it came down. As with any souls like experience, each step of the way was agony followed by pure bliss and admiration of (well not at the time for me but certainly now after having played some more Fromsoft games) yet another Myazaki masterpiece. 

The fast paced yet so immersive and cohesive nature of the game, flushed out mechanics and sheer compatibility with mods was outstanding. The joy I felt after beating Isshin is a feeling i will never forget. I was also streaming for my friends which made the anticipation and the victory even more rewarding as there was a shared sense of admiration, excitement and happiness. 

I also highly recommend the Resurrection overhaul mod. Incredibly well done, makes the game harder but in an actual challenging way and not just artificial boosting of enemy stats. When I say this game is accommodating for mods, I mean genuine NEW moves and COMBOS by bosses that require real time adaptation. Incredibly fun and rewarding, should u git gud and win...
